response.justification: This candidate is a full-stack web developer whose career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript, and DLang. Their C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — neither on Windows nor in a financial context — which falls orders of magnitude short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience, no fixed income or derivatives knowledge, and while a RIDE Capital stint exists, it involved fintech product engineering rather than capital-markets systems. None of the three mandatory requirements are met.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps background (GitLab, Docker, artifact management) aligns with the desirable pipeline knowledge
Java development history (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling) satisfies one of the optional desirable skills
Strong communication track record evidenced by public conference talks and multi-day workshops
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months, ~12 years ago) and entirely absent from the skills section — the single most critical mandatory requirement is unmet
No Windows platform development of any kind; entire career is Linux/cloud-native
No fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital-markets systems domain exposure — the required domain knowledge is missing
